title: Unable to init machine on aws EC2 Ubuntu 20.04
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**
/kind bug


**Description**

I am trying to run podman in AWS EC2. But after installation when I try to init machine it throws the following output

```bash
podman machine init
Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-36.20220918.2.2-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz: done
Killed
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  `sudo apt-get -qq --yes install podman`

2.
```
podman --version
podman version 3.4.2
```
3. `podman machine init`

**Describe the results you received:**
Machine was killed while initiating.


**Describe the results you expected:**
Machine to initiate successfully.



**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.4.2
```

**Output of `podman info`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
AWS - EC2 - t2.micro - 11GB Free space

comments.body: Question: Why are you trying to use `podman machine` on a Linux VM?

Hi I want to run some unkown codes through api.
Thus I was running them inside a container.

Hey,

Being a newbie I didn't knew that starting a machine is only required in `Windows` and `MacOS`.

For any future googlers if you are running podman on linux no need for 

❌ `podman machine init`
❌ `podman machine start`

After installing you can directly make image / run images using

```bash
podman build ...
podman run ...
```

> Being a newbie I didn't knew that starting a machine is only required in `Windows` and `MacOS`.

There seem to be a lot of new users of Docker Desktop (VM) on Linux, though. Even if not _required_.

----

I think the main issue was that you ran a VM in a VM ?

But `podman machine` itself should work on Linux too...
